title: hypericum maculatum subsp. quadrangulum
intro: Hypericum maculatum subsp. quadrangulum, also known as spotted St. John's Wort, is a plant species within the St. John's Wort genus, characterized by its distinctive spotted leaves and yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to exhibit antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits associated with Hypericum maculatum subsp. quadrangulum include its potential to treat wounds, reduce anxiety and stress, and alleviate symptoms of depression. The plant can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health issues.
